mcqs.question: How many threads are in one warp on an NVIDIA GPU?
mcqs.explanation: A warp on NVIDIA GPUs always consists of 32 threads that execute in lockstep under the SIMT model.

mcqs.question: Which memory type in the GPU hierarchy is the fastest and is private to each thread?
mcqs.explanation: Registers are on-chip, per-thread storage and represent the fastest memory on the GPU. Variables declared in kernel functions typically reside in registers.

mcqs.question: What happens when threads within a warp take different branches of an if-else statement?

options.text: The warp executes both branches in parallel
The warp serializes execution, running each branch with the other threads masked off
The divergent threads are moved to a different warp
The kernel is terminated with an error

mcqs.explanation: Warp divergence causes serialized execution: the warp first executes one branch with some threads masked, then the other branch with the remaining threads masked, reducing performance.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of shared memory in GPU architecture?

options.text: It stores data that is shared across all SMs on the GPU
It provides fast, on-chip scratchpad memory accessible by all threads within a thread block
It is the main off-chip DRAM visible to the CPU
It stores read-only constant values used by all kernels

mcqs.explanation: Shared memory is per-block, on-chip scratchpad memory that is much faster than global memory. It is declared with the __shared__ qualifier and used for inter-thread communication within a block.

mcqs.question: What is memory coalescing in GPU programming?

options.text: Combining multiple small memory allocations into one large allocation
Merging L1 and L2 cache entries
Combining consecutive memory accesses by threads in a warp into fewer memory transactions
Compressing data before storing in global memory

mcqs.explanation: Memory coalescing occurs when consecutive threads in a warp access consecutive global memory addresses, allowing the hardware to combine these into a single wide memory transaction, greatly improving bandwidth utilization.
